pairs.question: What was the distance and class structure of the 1984 Castrol 500 endurance race?
pairs.answer: The 1984 Castrol 500 was a 503 km endurance motor race held at Sandown Park circuit in Victoria, Australia on 9 September 1984. It featured two engine capacity classes for Group C Touring Cars: Up to 3000cc and Over 3000cc, along with a separate class for Group A cars, which were scheduled to replace Group C cars in Australian Touring Car racing the following year.

pairs.question: How did the Sandown Park circuit change for the 1984 Castrol 500 compared to previous races?
pairs.answer: The 1984 Castrol 500 introduced the new 3.878 km 'International Circuit' with an 800-meter infield section, crossing inside the venue's horse racing track for the first time. The pits were relocated to a new $600,000 pit complex at the start of the main straight, capable of accommodating over 40 cars with lock-up garages.

pairs.question: What was the significance of the 1984 Castrol 500 in terms of race distance and circuit history?
pairs.answer: The 1984 Castrol 500 was the first Sandown endurance race held at a 500 km distance on the new International Circuit. Previously, the race had been held at various distances (6 Hours, 3 Hours, 250 miles, and 400 km) on the older 3.100 km circuit from 1964 to 1983.

pairs.question: Why were teams required to have at least two drivers for the 1984 Castrol 500?
pairs.answer: Teams were required to have at least two drivers because the race distance of 503 km was too long for a single driver to complete without a break. This rule ensured driver safety and endurance.

pairs.question: What types of cars competed in the 1984 Castrol 500, and how did this reflect the transition in Australian Touring Car racing?
pairs.answer: The race included Group C Touring Cars in two engine capacity classes (Up to 3000cc and Over 3000cc) and a separate class for Group A cars. This reflected the transition from Group C to Group A cars, which were set to replace Group C in Australian Touring Car racing in 1985.

pairs.question: How many races were part of the 1984 Castrol 500 event at Sandown Park?
pairs.answer: The 1984 Castrol 500 was the first of five races held on the new 3.878 km 'International Circuit' at Sandown Park.

pairs.question: What was the total distance of the 1984 Castrol 500, and how did it compare to the previous Sandown Enduro races?
pairs.answer: The 1984 Castrol 500 covered a total distance of 503 km. Previous Sandown Enduro races had distances ranging from 6 Hours (1964–65) to 400 km (1976–83), all held on the older 3.100 km circuit.
